title: एकादशी माहात्म्य
devanagariText:
॥ श्री एकादशी माहात्म्यम् ॥

ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय॥

॥ एकादशी स्तुति ॥
एकादश्यां निराहारो व्रतं कुर्यात्समाहितः।
विष्णुप्रीतिकरं ह्येतत् सर्वपापप्रणाशनम्॥

श्रवणद्वादशीयुक्ता पञ्चदश्या समन्विता।
एकादशी महापुण्या सर्वकामप्रदायिनी॥

॥ एकादशी उत्पत्ति कथा ॥
एकदा ब्रह्मणा पृष्टो भगवान् विष्णुरव्ययः।
कथयामास धर्मज्ञो एकादश्याः समुत्पत्तिम्॥

सत्ययुगे महाघोरो दैत्यो मुरासुरो महान्।
देवान् जित्वा स्वर्गलोकं प्राप्य तत्र प्रशासनम्॥

करोति देवताः सर्वाः भीताः शरणमागताः।
विष्णोः पुरः प्रणम्यैव प्रार्थयन्तोऽसुरक्षयम्॥

भगवान् विष्णुः मुरासुरेण सह युद्धं कृत्वा
बद्रिकाश्रमे सिंहवत्यां शयनं कृतवान्।
मुरासुरो भगवन्तं सुप्तं दृष्ट्वा मारयितुमैच्छत्।
तदा भगवतः शरीरात् एका दिव्या कन्या प्रादुर्भूता
या मुरासुरं हतवती॥

भगवान् प्रसन्नो भूत्वा तामुवाच —
त्वमेकादशी नाम्ना विश्वे विख्याता भविष्यसि।
मम प्रिया तिथिर्भूत्वा सर्वपापनाशिनी भव।
ये मानवास्त्वदा व्रतं कुर्वन्ति ते मम प्रियाः॥

॥ एकादशी व्रत विधि ॥
दशम्यां सायंकाले एकभुक्तं कुर्यात्।
एकादश्यां पूर्णोपवासं कुर्यात् अथवा फलाहारं कुर्यात्।
चावल, अन्न, दाल, प्याज, लहसुन वर्जित हैं।
भगवान विष्णु का पूजन, नाम-जप और कथा-श्रवण करें।
रात्रि जागरण विशेष फलदायी है।
द्वादशी को पारणा (व्रत खोलना) करें॥

॥ एकादशी महिमा ॥
ब्रह्महत्यादिपापानि गोहत्यासमभानि च।
एकादशीव्रतेनैव नश्यन्ति नात्र संशयः॥

न गायत्र्याः परो मन्त्रो न मातुः परदैवतम्।
न काश्याः परमं तीर्थं न एकादश्याः परं व्रतम्॥

अश्वमेधसहस्राणि राजसूयशतानि च।
एकादश्युपवासस्य कलां नार्हन्ति षोडशीम्॥

॥ चौबीस एकादशियाँ ॥
पौष कृष्ण — पुत्रदा एकादशी
पौष शुक्ल — षट्तिला एकादशी
माघ कृष्ण — जया एकादशी
माघ शुक्ल — विजया एकादशी
फाल्गुन कृष्ण — आमलकी एकादशी
फाल्गुन शुक्ल — पापमोचनी एकादशी
चैत्र कृष्ण — कामदा एकादशी
चैत्र शुक्ल — वरूथिनी एकादशी
वैशाख कृष्ण — मोहिनी एकादशी
वैशाख शुक्ल — अपरा एकादशी
ज्येष्ठ कृष्ण — निर्जला एकादशी
ज्येष्ठ शुक्ल — योगिनी एकादशी
आषाढ़ कृष्ण — देवशयनी एकादशी
आषाढ़ शुक्ल — कामिका एकादशी
श्रावण कृष्ण — पुत्रदा एकादशी
श्रावण शुक्ल — अजा एकादशी
भाद्रपद कृष्ण — पद्मा (परिवर्तिनी) एकादशी
भाद्रपद शुक्ल — इन्दिरा एकादशी
आश्विन कृष्ण — पापांकुशा एकादशी
आश्विन शुक्ल — रमा एकादशी
कार्तिक कृष्ण — देवउठनी (प्रबोधिनी) एकादशी
कार्तिक शुक्ल — उत्पन्ना एकादशी
मार्गशीर्ष कृष्ण — मोक्षदा एकादशी
मार्गशीर्ष शुक्ल — सफला एकादशी

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
एकादशीमाहात्म्यं यः पठेत् शृणुयात् तथा।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्री एकादशी माहात्म्यं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ekadashi Mahatmyam ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya.

|| Ekadashi Stuti ||
Ekadashyam Niraharo Vratam Kuryat Samahitah.
Vishnu-priti-karam Hy-etat Sarva-papa-pranashanam.

Shravana-dvadashi-yukta Panchadashya Samanvita.
Ekadashi Mahapunya Sarva-kama-pradayini.

|| Ekadashi Utpatti Katha ||
Ekada Brahmana Prishtho Bhagavan Vishnur-avyayah.
Kathayamasa Dharmajno Ekadashyah Samutpattim.

Satya-yuge Mahaghoro Daityo Murasuro Mahan.
Devan Jitva Svargalokam Prapya Tatra Prashasanam.

Karoti Devatah Sarvah Bhitah Sharanam-agatah.
Vishnoh Purah Pranamyaiva Prarthayanto-asura-kshayam.

Bhagavan Vishnuh Murasurena Saha Yuddham Kritva
Badrikashrame Simhavatyam Shayanam Kritavan.
Murasuro Bhagavantam Suptam Drishtva Marayitum-aichchhat.
Tada Bhagavatah Sharirat Eka Divya Kanya Pradurbhuta
Ya Murasuram Hatavati.

Bhagavan Prasanno Bhutva Tam-uvacha —
Tvam-Ekadashi Namna Vishve Vikhyata Bhavishyasi.
Mama Priya Tithir-bhutva Sarva-papa-nashini Bhava.
Ye Manavaas-tvada Vratam Kurvanti Te Mama Priyah.

|| Ekadashi Vrat Vidhi ||
Dashamyam Sayamkale Ekabhuktam Kuryat.
Ekadashyam Purna-upvasam Kuryat Athva Phalaharam Kuryat.
Chaval, Anna, Dal, Pyaj, Lahsun Varjit Hain.
Bhagwan Vishnu Ka Pujan, Nama-japa Aur Katha-shravana Karen.
Ratri Jagaran Vishesh Phaldayi Hai.
Dvadashi Ko Parana (Vrat Kholna) Karen.

|| Ekadashi Mahima ||
Brahma-hatya-adi-papani Gohatya-samabhani Cha.
Ekadashi-vrata-enaiva Nashyanti Natra Samshayah.

Na Gayatryah Paro Mantro Na Matuh Para-daivatam.
Na Kashyah Paramam Tirtham Na Ekadashyah Param Vratam.

Ashvamedha-sahasrani Rajsuya-shatani Cha.
Ekadashi-upavasasya Kalam Na-arhanti Shodashim.

|| Chaubis Ekadashiyan ||
Paush Krishna — Putrada Ekadashi
Paush Shukla — Shattila Ekadashi
Magh Krishna — Jaya Ekadashi
Magh Shukla — Vijaya Ekadashi
Phalgun Krishna — Amalaki Ekadashi
Phalgun Shukla — Papamochani Ekadashi
Chaitra Krishna — Kamada Ekadashi
Chaitra Shukla — Varuthini Ekadashi
Vaishakh Krishna — Mohini Ekadashi
Vaishakh Shukla — Apara Ekadashi
Jyeshtha Krishna — Nirjala Ekadashi
Jyeshtha Shukla — Yogini Ekadashi
Ashadh Krishna — Devshayani Ekadashi
Ashadh Shukla — Kamika Ekadashi
Shravan Krishna — Putrada Ekadashi
Shravan Shukla — Aja Ekadashi
Bhadrapad Krishna — Padma (Parivartini) Ekadashi
Bhadrapad Shukla — Indira Ekadashi
Ashvin Krishna — Papankusha Ekadashi
Ashvin Shukla — Rama Ekadashi
Kartik Krishna — Devuthani (Prabodhini) Ekadashi
Kartik Shukla — Utpanna Ekadashi
Margashirsha Krishna — Mokshada Ekadashi
Margashirsha Shukla — Saphala Ekadashi

|| Phalashruti ||
Ekadashi-mahatmyam Yah Pathet Shrinuyat Tatha.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnu-lokam Sa Gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Ekadashi Mahatmyam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
एकादशी माहात्म्य में एकादशी तिथि की उत्पत्ति, महिमा और व्रत विधि का वर्णन है।

उत्पत्ति कथा के अनुसार सत्ययुग में मुरासुर नामक दैत्य ने देवताओं को परास्त कर स्वर्ग पर अधिकार कर लिया। भगवान विष्णु ने मुरासुर से युद्ध किया और बद्रिकाश्रम में विश्राम किया। जब मुरासुर ने सोते हुए भगवान पर आक्रमण किया तो भगवान के शरीर से एक दिव्य कन्या प्रकट हुई जिसने मुरासुर का वध किया। भगवान ने प्रसन्न होकर उसे 'एकादशी' नाम दिया और कहा कि यह तिथि मेरी प्रिय होगी।

एकादशी व्रत की महिमा इतनी अधिक है कि सहस्र अश्वमेध यज्ञ और सौ राजसूय यज्ञ भी एकादशी उपवास की सोलहवीं कला के बराबर नहीं हैं।

वर्ष में 24 एकादशियाँ होती हैं, प्रत्येक का विशेष नाम और महत्त्व है। निर्जला एकादशी सबसे कठिन और सबसे फलदायी मानी जाती है।
benefits: Comprehensive guide to Ekadashi, the most important Vaishnava fasting day
Origin story of Ekadashi from Lord Vishnu's divine power
Single Ekadashi fast exceeds merit of thousands of Ashvamedha Yagyas
Traditionally recited for removal of even the gravest sins including brahmahatya
Lists all 24 Ekadashis of the year with their names and months
Night vigil (Ratri Jagaran) on Ekadashi multiplies merit manifold
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Ekadashi Mahatmya (एकादशी माहात्म्य) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Padma Purana - Uttara Khanda. Devotees recite this prayer to list all 24 Ekadashis of the year with their names and months and origin story of Ekadashi from Lord Vishnu's divine power. It is also said to night vigil (Ratri Jagaran) on Ekadashi multiplies merit manifold. This prayer is traditionally recited during Ekadashi to invoke divine grace. At 15 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 15 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Ekadashi Mahatmya in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ekadashi Mahatmya in Sanskrit and Hindi. Complete guide to Ekadashi fasting with origin story, all 24 Ekadashi names, and vrat vidhi.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ekadashi Mahatmya?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ekadashi Mahatmya (एकादशी माहात्म्यम्) is a comprehensive scriptural treatise from the Padma Purana Uttara Khanda detailing the supreme glory (mahatmya) of the Ekadashi vrata — the 11th lunar tithi observed twice every month as the most sacred fast in Vaishnava tradition. It contains the Ekadashi Stuti (foundational hymn), the Ekadashi Utpatti Katha (origin story of the goddess Ekadashi from Vishnu's body to slay Murasura), the Ekadashi Vrata Vidhi (precise rules of observance — dashami fast preparation, ekadashi total fast or fruit fast, dvadashi parana), the Ekadashi Mahima (24 verses comparing Ekadashi merit to Ashvamedha and Rajasuya yajnas), the Chaubis Ekadashi list (all 24 annual Ekadashi names with their lunar months) and the concluding phala-shruti. (Source: Padma Purana — Uttara Khanda.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the origin story (utpatti katha) of Ekadashi?
seoFaqs.answer: The Padma Purana narrates: In Satya-yuga the asura Murasura (a powerful demon-son of Talajangha) defeated all the devas and seized rule of svarga-loka. Terrified, the gods sought refuge with Bhagavan Vishnu, Who waged a long battle with Murasura and finally retreated to the cave Simhavati in Badrikashrama for rest. Murasura followed the Lord intending to kill Him in His sleep. As the demon raised his weapon, a divine kanya emerged from Vishnu's body — radiant, weapon-bearing — who instantly slew Murasura. Pleased, Vishnu blessed her: Tvam Ekadashi namna vishve vikhyata bhavishyasi — you shall be known throughout the universe as Ekadashi (since you arose on the eleventh tithi); you shall be My priya tithi, the destroyer of all sins; whoever observes vrata on your day will be My beloved devotee. (Source: Padma Purana Uttara Khanda; Ekadashi Utpatti Katha.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the 24 Ekadashis of the year and their names?
seoFaqs.answer: The Padma Purana lists 24 Ekadashis (two per lunar month — Krishna paksha and Shukla paksha): Paush — Putrada and Shattila; Magh — Jaya and Vijaya; Phalgun — Amalaki and Papamochani; Chaitra — Kamada and Varuthini; Vaishakh — Mohini and Apara; Jyeshtha — Nirjala and Yogini; Ashadh — Devshayani and Kamika; Shravan — Putrada (second) and Aja; Bhadrapad — Padma (Parivartini) and Indira; Ashvin — Papankusha and Rama; Kartik — Devuthani / Prabodhini and Utpanna; Margashirsha — Mokshada and Saphala. In adhika-masa years two extra Ekadashis (Padmini in Shukla and Parama in Krishna) are added, totaling 26. Each Ekadashi has its own specific katha and unique blessing — for example Putrada for progeny, Mokshada for liberation, Vaikuntha (= Mokshada) for opening the gates of Vaikuntha. (Sources: Padma Purana Uttara Khanda; Drikpanchang Ekadashi calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the precise Ekadashi vrat vidhi (procedure)?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ekadashi Vrat Vidhi has four stages: (1) Dashami evening — Ekabhukta (single meal) of sattvic food without rice and pulses, sleeping early. (2) Ekadashi day — Brahmamuhurta snana, sankalpa to observe the vrata, full fast (purna upavasa) for the strong-willed or phalahara (only fruits, milk, water, root vegetables) for the household; rice, grain, dal, onion, garlic and tamasic foods are completely forbidden; the day is spent in Vishnu pujan, Vishnu Sahasranama or Vishnu naam-japa, Bhagavata-katha shravana and tulsi-archana. (3) Ekadashi night — Ratri Jagaran (night vigil) is held to multiply merit manifold, with Vishnu bhajan, Hari-katha and Vishnu Sahasranama. (4) Dvadashi morning — after sunrise but within the parana muhurta (dvadashi-tithi while it persists), the vrata is broken with a small offering of food blessed by Vishnu, beginning with tulsi-water. (Source: Padma Purana Ekadashi Vrat Vidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is night vigil (Ratri Jagaran) emphasized on Ekadashi?
seoFaqs.answer: Ratri Jagaran (रात्रि-जागरण — staying awake the entire night of Ekadashi) is described in the Ekadashi Mahatmya as multiplying the merit of the day-fast manifold. The reasoning is: Ekadashi tithi spans approximately 24 hours and the vow continues until dvadashi parana the next morning; sleep on Ekadashi night allows the indriyas and mind to fall into tamas and break the spirit of the vrata. Jagaran with Vishnu bhajan, Bhagavata Mahapurana parayana, Vishnu Sahasranama, Hari-naam sankirtan and Bhagavad Gita parayana keeps the prana focused on Hari throughout the entire Hari-vasara. The famous bhajan-jagarans of Tirupati Vaikuntha Ekadashi, the Pandharpur Ashadhi Ekadashi (Devshayani) wari, the Vrindavan Mokshada Ekadashi night kirtans, and the Nirjala Ekadashi watery vigils are all built on this Mahatmya-prescribed jagaran tradition. (Sources: Padma Purana Ekadashi Mahatmya; Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Ekadashi Mahatmya and the Ekadashi Mahatmya Shloka?
seoFaqs.answer: Both texts originate from the Padma Purana but differ in scope. The Ekadashi Mahatmya Shloka (एकादशी महात्म्य श्लोक) is a concise 8-verse selection of the most quoted scriptural-authority verses establishing why Ekadashi is supreme — used as the opening pramana before any Ekadashi observance. The full Ekadashi Mahatmyam (एकादशी माहात्म्यम्) is a much more extensive treatise containing the foundational stuti, the complete Ekadashi Utpatti Katha (origin from Vishnu's body), detailed vrata-vidhi for all four stages of observance, the 24 Ekadashi names with lunar months, the comparative-merit shlokas, the rules of dvadashi parana and the concluding phala-shruti. Devotees recite the shlokas on every Ekadashi morning and the full Mahatmya during major Ekadashis like Vaikuntha, Nirjala and Mokshada. (Sources: Padma Purana Uttara Khanda; Vaishnava Ekadashi paddhati.)
